repos.brief:
**What it does:** OmniRoute is a self-hostable TypeScript gateway that exposes a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint routing requests across 237 AI providers, with 90+ free tiers, automatic fallback, and RTK+Caveman prompt compression that cuts token usage by up to 95%.

**Why now:** The repo gained nearly 5,000 stars this week, signaling rapid community adoption, and the timing aligns with Gemini CLI and Codex CLI launches that created fresh demand for unified, cost-free inference routing across competing tools.

**Build with it:** Point Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, or any OpenAI SDK client at your local OmniRoute instance to get free, uninterrupted coding assistance that automatically fails over across Claude, GPT, and Gemini when rate limits hit—no code changes required, just swap the base URL.

**What it does:** herdr is a Rust-based terminal multiplexer that runs multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) simultaneously in real terminal panes, with automatic state detection showing which agents are blocked, working, or idle.

**Why now:** It hit #1 GitHub Trending this week with 4,552 stars in a single week, signaling the builder community is actively solving the "running multiple agents in parallel" workflow problem right now.

**Build with it:** Use the socket API to build an orchestration layer that spawns specialized agents per microservice or file domain, monitors their blocked/done states programmatically, and automatically routes follow-up prompts to unblock stalled agents without human intervention.

**What it does:** Ponytail is a JavaScript plugin for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) that enforces a "laziest senior dev" heuristic—biasing the agent toward using built-in browser/platform primitives and deleting code rather than adding it, measurably producing ~54% fewer lines of code on real repos.

**Why now:** The repo gained 8,085 stars this week, signaling rapid adoption at the moment developers are actively building agentic coding workflows and feeling the pain of AI agents that over-engineer simple tasks.

**Build with it:** Drop the skill into your Claude Code or Cursor setup to ship leaner AI-assisted features faster—especially useful if you're scaffolding new SaaS products and want your agent to reach for `<input type="date">` instead of pulling in flatpickr.

**What it does:** Caveman is a prompt skill/plugin that instructs AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, and 30+ others) to strip filler language from responses, cutting output tokens by ~65% while leaving code, commands, and error messages untouched.

**Why now:** It hit 86,000+ GitHub stars this week with 7,940 new stars, signaling widespread pain around verbose AI agent output and API token costs at scale.

**Build with it:** Drop the install script into your dev container setup or CI environment to reduce token spend on every agentic coding session, or fork the prompt levels to build a configurable verbosity-control layer for your own LLM-powered tool.

**What it does:** Taste-Skill is a collection of portable agent skills (markdown instruction files) that you drop into Codex, Cursor, or Claude Code to enforce opinionated layout, typography, spacing, and motion rules, steering AI away from generic boilerplate UI output.

**Why now:** It pulled 6,277 stars this week, likely riding the wave of Claude Code and Codex going mainstream, where developers are now hitting the ceiling of AI-generated UI quality and actively looking for ways to raise the output bar.

**Build with it:** Run `npx skills add https://github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-skill` to inject the design skill into your agent workflow, then use the bundled image-generation skills to produce reference boards in ChatGPT Images and hand those frames directly to your coding agent as visual specs.

**What it does:** Agent Reach is a Python CLI and MCP toolkit that gives AI agents authenticated, scrape-ready access to Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu, and more — routing around paywalled APIs and bot-blocking by managing cookies, browser sessions, and fallback backends automatically.

**Why now:** The project hit GitHub's #1 trending repository this week with 5,890 new stars, driven by its real-time pivot from yt-dlp (blocked by Bilibili's anti-bot systems) to bili-cli — demonstrating active maintenance exactly when platform lock-out is accelerating.

**Build with it:** Drop it into a Claude Code or Cursor workflow to let your agent autonomously research competitors on Twitter, summarize Reddit bug threads, or pull YouTube transcripts — without writing a single scraper or paying for a single API key.

**What it does:** codebase-memory-mcp is a single static binary MCP server that indexes entire codebases into a persistent SQLite knowledge graph using tree-sitter AST parsing across 158 languages, answering structural queries about functions, call chains, classes, and HTTP routes in under 1ms with zero dependencies.

**Why now:** The project gained 5,474 GitHub stars this week, signaling rapid adoption across AI coding agent workflows, and it now supports 11 agents including Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and Gemini CLI with a published arXiv benchmark showing 83% answer quality at 10× fewer tokens than file-by-file exploration.

**Build with it:** Drop this into any AI coding agent setup to give your LLM a persistent, queryable map of a large monorepo or unfamiliar codebase — enabling agents to navigate cross-service call chains, resolve type dependencies, and answer structural questions without burning context on raw file reads.

**What it does:** OpenMontage is an open-source agentic video production system that gives your AI coding assistant 12 pipelines and 52 tools to handle scripting, asset generation, editing, and final render — producing real motion-clip videos, not just animated stills.

**Why now:** The repo hit #1 on GitHub Trending this week with 5,378 new stars, signaling a sharp spike in developer attention right as agentic tooling and text-to-video models are maturing enough to be practically useful together.

**Build with it:** Drop in your own footage pipeline or wire up a new provider (Kling, Veo, Stable Diffusion) using the existing tool scaffolding, and ship a domain-specific video agent — think automated product demos, news recap reels, or training content generators — for under $2 a video.

**What it does:** This repo collects and publishes extracted system prompts from major AI products—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, Copilot, and others—updated regularly as new model versions ship.

**Why now:** Claude Sonnet 5 and Claude Design's full prompt (including 48 tools, 16 skills, and 9 starter sources) dropped this week, giving builders an unusually detailed look at how Anthropic structures agentic workflows at production scale.

**Build with it:** Use the Claude Design and Claude Code prompts as reference architecture to design your own multi-tool agent systems, or diff model versions (e.g., Opus 4.8 → Fable 5) to understand which behavioral constraints are hardcoded versus tunable before building on top of these APIs.

**What it does:** /last30days is a Python-based AI agent skill that searches Reddit, X, YouTube, TikTok, HackerNews, Polymarket, and the web in parallel, scores results by real engagement signals (upvotes, likes, real-money prediction market odds), and synthesizes a single grounded summary of what's actually happening on any topic in the past 30 days.

**Why now:** It hit #1 on GitHub Trending this week with 2,393 stars in seven days, signaling strong builder demand for recency-aware research that bypasses the stale training data and walled-garden limitations of ChatGPT, Gemini, and native Claude.

**Build with it:** Drop it into Claude Code or any of 50+ agent hosts to add a live, multi-platform research layer to your app — useful for pre-call intelligence tools, trend dashboards, competitive monitoring, or any product where "what happened this month" matters more than what a search index cached last quarter.
